Manipulation check analysis

0. Rationale

This file presents the results of the manipulation check experiment.

We begin by importing the raw data and creating a dataset. The extract function retrieves the specified data from each raw file and compiles the results into a single dataset, shown below.

Show the code 
rm(list=ls())
set.seed(2026)

library(tidyverse)  # dplyr, tidyr, ggplot2
library(janitor)
library(DT)

# CSV original column names
singleton_orig <- c("PROLIFIC_PID", "date", "OS",
  "tempo_consenso", "InstTot.started", "InstTot.stopped",
  "QUEST.block_1/AGE", "QUEST.block_1/SEX", "QUEST.block_1/Education level", "Ant_lastquestion.stopped")
trial_orig <- c("tempo_Testo", "condizione",
  "tempo_vd1",   "previous.response", "tempo_prob1", "previous_2.response", 
  "tempo_prob2", "previous_3.response")
n_trials <- 3
singleton_clean <- janitor::make_clean_names(singleton_orig)
trial_clean     <- janitor::make_clean_names(trial_orig)
singleton_labels <- singleton_clean
trial_labels     <- unlist(lapply(trial_clean, function(v)
  paste0(v, "_trial", seq_len(n_trials))))

all_labels <- c(singleton_labels, trial_labels)

# Extraction function
extract <- function(file_path) {
  dataset <- tryCatch(read.csv(file_path, 
                               stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
                               check.names = FALSE),
    error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(dataset))
    return(setNames(rep(NA, length(all_labels)), all_labels))
  
  colnames(dataset) <- janitor::make_clean_names(colnames(dataset))
  
  result <- list()
    for (i in seq_along(singleton_clean)) {
    v     <- singleton_clean[i]
    label <- singleton_labels[i]
    if (v %in% colnames(dataset)) {
      non_na <- dataset[[v]][!is.na(dataset[[v]]) & dataset[[v]] != ""]
      result[[label]] <- if (length(non_na) > 0) non_na[1] else NA
    } else {
      result[[label]] <- NA
    }
  }
    for (i in seq_along(trial_clean)) {
    v      <- trial_clean[i]
    non_na <- if (v %in% colnames(dataset))
      dataset[[v]][!is.na(dataset[[v]]) & dataset[[v]] != ""]
    else character(0)
    padded     <- rep(NA_character_, n_trials)
    take       <- min(length(non_na), n_trials)
    if (take > 0) padded[seq_len(take)] <- as.character(non_na[seq_len(take)])
    for (t in seq_len(n_trials))
      result[[paste0(v, "_trial", t)]] <- padded[t]
  }
  
  return(unlist(result[all_labels]))
}

# Raw data folder
file_paths <- list.files(
  path        = file.path(getwd(), "RAW12_ANON"),
  pattern     = "\\.csv$",
  full.names  = TRUE
)
if (length(file_paths) == 0)
  stop("No CSV files found.")

results  <- lapply(file_paths, extract)
dfa_wide <- as.data.frame(do.call(rbind, results), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
colnames(dfa_wide) <- all_labels
dfa_wide <- cbind(source_file = basename(file_paths), dfa_wide)

singleton_numeric <- intersect(names(dfa_wide), c(
  "tempo_consenso", "insttot_started", "insttot_stopped",
  "quest_block_1_age", "quest_block_1_education_level",
  "ant_lastquestion_stopped"
))
trial_numeric <- grep("^(tempo_|previous)", names(dfa_wide), value = TRUE)
numeric_cols <- unique(c(singleton_numeric, trial_numeric))
dfa_wide[numeric_cols] <- lapply(dfa_wide[numeric_cols], function(x) as.numeric(as.character(x)))
char_cols <- grep("^condizione_|^quest_block_1_sex$", names(dfa_wide),
                  value = TRUE)
dfa_wide[char_cols] <- lapply(dfa_wide[char_cols], function(x) {
  tolower(trimws(as.character(x)))
})
dfa_long <- dfa_wide %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = all_of(trial_labels),
    names_to  = c(".value", "trial_num"),
    names_pattern = "^(.+)_(trial\\d+)$"
  ) %>%
  mutate(trial_num = as.integer(sub("trial", "", trial_num))) %>%
  relocate(source_file, prolific_pid, trial_num, condizione)

# Long dataset
dt_long <- datatable(
  dfa_long,
  caption = "Long dataset (1 row per trial)",
  options = list(scrollX = "400px", scrollY = "300px",
                 scrollCollapse = TRUE, pageLength = 5, autoWidth = TRUE),
  class = "cell-border stripe compact"
) |> formatStyle(names(dfa_long), lineHeight = ".3em", padding = "1px")
dt_long

1. Exclusion criteria

A participant was excluded entirely if, in at least one of their three trials, they violated any of the following criteria:

  1. tempo_testo < 5 sec — read the initial story implausibly fast.
  2. Duration of the general instructions screen < 5 sec — completed the instructions implausibly fast.
  3. tempo_vd1 < 2 sec — responded too quickly on the first dependent variable.
  4. tempo_prob1 < 2 sec — responded too quickly on the second dependent variable.
  5. tempo_prob2 < 2 sec — responded too quickly on the third dependent variable.
  6. os == "Phone" — device not suitable.
Show the code 
dfa_wide_raw <- dfa_wide
dfa_long_raw <- dfa_long

exclusion_flags <- dfa_long_raw %>%
  mutate(.durata_istruzioni = as.numeric(inst_tot_stopped) - 
           as.numeric(inst_tot_started)) %>%
  group_by(prolific_pid) %>%
  summarise(
    flag_tempo_testo = any(tempo_testo  < 5, na.rm = TRUE),
    flag_istruzioni   = any(.durata_istruzioni < 5, na.rm = TRUE),
    flag_tempo_vd1    = any(tempo_vd1   < 2, na.rm = TRUE),
    flag_tempo_prob1  = any(tempo_prob1 < 2, na.rm = TRUE),
    flag_tempo_prob2  = any(tempo_prob2 < 2, na.rm = TRUE),
    flag_iphone       = any(os == "iPhone", na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    excluded = flag_tempo_testo | flag_istruzioni | flag_tempo_vd1 |
      flag_tempo_prob1 | flag_tempo_prob2 | flag_iphone
  )

exclusion_report <- exclusion_flags %>% filter(excluded)

summary_counts <- data.frame(
  Metric = c("Total participants", "Participants excluded", "Participants remaining"),
  Value = c(n_distinct(dfa_long_raw$prolific_pid), sum(exclusion_flags$excluded), sum(!exclusion_flags$excluded))
)
knitr::kable(summary_counts, caption = "Exclusion Criteria")
Exclusion Criteria
Metric Value
Total participants 71
Participants excluded 6
Participants remaining 65
Show the code 
excluded_pids <- exclusion_flags$prolific_pid[exclusion_flags$excluded]

# Riscriviamo i dataset filtrando via i soggetti esclusi
dfa_wide <- dfa_wide_raw %>% filter(!prolific_pid %in% excluded_pids)
dfa_long <- dfa_long_raw %>% filter(!prolific_pid %in% excluded_pids)

n_partecipanti_post_esclusione <- n_distinct(dfa_wide$prolific_pid)

2. Participants description

The final sample, after applying the exclusion criteria, included 65 participants.

2.1 Biological sex

We visualized the distribution of biological sex across the final sample.

Show the code 
knitr::kable(table(dfa_wide$quest_block_1_sex))
Var1 Freq
fe 33
ma 32
Show the code 
ggplot(dfa_wide, aes(x = quest_block_1_sex, fill = quest_block_1_sex)) +
  geom_bar(color = "black") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  labs(x = "Biological sex", y = "Count", title = "") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

2.2 Age

We first calculate descriptive statistics and then visualize the distribution.

Show the code 
knitr::kable(t(summary(dfa_wide$quest_block_1_age)))
Min. 1st Qu. Median Mean 3rd Qu. Max.
18 21 22 21.96923 23 24
Show the code 
ggplot(dfa_wide, aes(x = quest_block_1_age)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, color = "black", fill = "darkgreen") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  labs(x = "Age", y = "Count", title = "")

2.3 Education

We first calculate descriptive statistics and then visualize the distribution (3 = High School, 4 = Bachelor Degree, 5 = Master Degree).

Show the code 
knitr::kable(table(dfa_wide$quest_block_1_education_level))
Var1 Freq
3 27
4 37
5 1
Show the code 
ggplot(dfa_wide, aes(x = factor(quest_block_1_education_level))) +
  geom_bar(color = "black", fill = "darkgreen") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  labs(x = "Education level", y = "Count", title = "")

3. Responses

For each of the three response variables below, we first calculate descriptive statistics by condition and then visualize the distribution.

3.1 Prior knowledge

previous_response captures participants’ prior knowledge of the causal relationship.

Show the code 
tab_aspettativa_priori <- dfa_long %>%
  group_by(condizione) %>%
  summarise(
    Media = round(mean(previous_response, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    SD    = round(sd(previous_response, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    `% responses >50` = paste0(round(mean(previous_response > 50,
                                         na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 1), "%"),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  rename(Condizione = condizione)

knitr::kable(tab_aspettativa_priori, caption = "Prior knowledge", align = "c")
Prior knowledge
Condizione Media SD % responses >50
a 52.46 23.54 43.1%
l 70.22 17.77 81.5%
m 68.66 19.33 78.5%
Show the code 
ggplot(dfa_long, aes(x = condizione, y = previous_response, fill = condizione)) +
  geom_violin(alpha = 0.35, trim = FALSE, color = NA) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.15, outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.8, color = "black") +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.08, alpha = 0.3, size = 1, color = "black") +
  ylim(c(0, 100)) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  labs(x = "Condition", y = "Response", title = "") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

3.2 Deterministic judgment (when the cause is present)

previous_2_response captures participants’ deterministic judgment (when the candidate cause is present).

Show the code 
tab_causa_presente <- dfa_long %>%
  group_by(condizione) %>%
  summarise(
    Media = round(mean(previous_2_response, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    SD    = round(sd(previous_2_response, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    `% responses >95` = paste0(round(mean(previous_2_response > 95,
                                         na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 1), "%"),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  rename(Condizione = condizione)

knitr::kable(tab_causa_presente, caption = "Deterministic judgment (i)",
             align = "c")
Deterministic judgment (i)
Condizione Media SD % responses >95
a 80.11 21.15 30.8%
l 87.02 17.51 40%
m 84.72 14.18 24.6%
Show the code 
ggplot(dfa_long, aes(x = condizione, y = previous_2_response, 
                     fill = condizione)) +
  geom_violin(alpha = 0.35, trim = FALSE, color = NA) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.15, outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.8, color = "black") +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.08, alpha = 0.3, size = 1, color = "black") +
  ylim(c(0, 100)) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  labs(x = "Condition", y = "Response", title = "") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

3.3 Deterministic judgment (when the cause is absent)

previous_3_response captures participants’ deterministic judgment (when the candidate cause is absent).

Show the code 
tab_causa_assente <- dfa_long %>%
  group_by(condizione) %>%
  summarise(
    Media = round(mean(previous_3_response, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    SD    = round(sd(previous_3_response, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    `% responses <5` = paste0(round(mean(previous_3_response < 5, 
                                        na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 1), "%"),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  rename(Condizione = condizione)

knitr::kable(tab_causa_assente, caption = "Deterministic judgment (ii)", 
             align = "c")
Deterministic judgment (ii)
Condizione Media SD % responses <5
a 16.49 25.24 44.6%
l 17.25 25.48 46.2%
m 18.00 19.17 24.6%
Show the code 
ggplot(dfa_long, aes(x = condizione, y = previous_3_response, fill = condizione)) +
  geom_violin(alpha = 0.35, trim = FALSE, color = NA) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.15, outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.8, color = "black") +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.08, alpha = 0.3, size = 1, color = "black") +
  ylim(c(0, 100)) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  labs(x = "Condition", y = "Response", title = "") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
